name: Marinated Beef Province Style
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the meat into 2-inch cubes.
2. Dice the bacon.
3. Skin and crush the garlic using a firm knife or garlic press; blend with chopped parsley, ground cloves, and allspice.
4. Place the meat in a dish and add the red wine vinegar, red wine, and garlic mixture.
5. Peel and thinly slice one third of the onions and carrots. Add to the marinade with salt and pepper. Let marinate for at least 12 hours, stirring occasionally.
6. Peel the remaining onions and carrots. Heat oil in a large heavy skillet. Fry diced bacon and sliced onions until golden brown.
7. Transfer meat from the marinade, draining well. Brown the meat in the pan's remaining oil and add to the onions. Pour the marinade liquid and vegetables into the skillet. Stir to absorb the oil.
8. Add whole carrots, brandy, and herbs.
9. Squeeze orange zest and a little juice into the sauce. Season with salt and pepper.
10. Pour the sauce over meat and onions. Cover the casserole.
11. Cook in the center of a slow oven (350°F) for 2 1/4 hours. Serve garnish removed, often paired with spaghetti, macaroni, or noodles topped with butter, grated cheese, and chopped parsley.
